skill.name: ios-developer
skill.description: Develop native iOS applications with Swift/SwiftUI. Masters iOS 18, SwiftUI, UIKit integration, Core Data, networking, and App Store optimization. Use PROACTIVELY for iOS-specific features, App Store optimization, or native iOS development.
skill.summary: Guide native iOS app development with Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit, testing, performance, privacy, and App Store readiness.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported 10 potential issues, but all reviewed matches are false positives in Markdown prose. No executable code, real shell invocation, credential access, weak cryptography implementation, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line references opening a local resource document in Markdown inline-code formatting. It does not execute a command or invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is a backticked Markdown file path, not a Ruby or shell execution construct. No command execution syntax appears on the line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keychain Mention Is Security Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines list Keychain Services as an iOS security capability. They do not access a local macOS Keychain or extract credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a capabilities list for secure iOS data storage. There is no executable code, command, API call, or instruction to retrieve secrets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Prose
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain iOS description text, accessibility topics, design guidance, and response approach items. They do not implement or recommend weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All matched locations are plain English skill descriptions or accessibility guidance. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Matched UIKit Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line discusses UIKit integration for iOS development. It does not instruct the agent to enumerate host system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is an iOS framework capability list. There is no command, file path, environment inspection, or host reconnaissance instruction.

content.user_title: Build Native iOS Apps with SwiftUI
content.value_statement: Modern iOS work requires platform knowledge across SwiftUI, UIKit, privacy, testing, and App Store rules. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan, review, and implement native iOS features with practical guidance.
actual_capabilities: Plans SwiftUI-first iOS features with UIKit integration when needed.
Explains architecture options such as MVVM, coordinators, repositories, and dependency injection.
Guides Core Data, SwiftData, CloudKit, Keychain, and offline data workflows.
Recommends testing approaches using XCTest, XCUITest, snapshots, and TestFlight.
Covers performance, accessibility, privacy, and App Store review considerations.
limitations: Does not run Xcode builds or execute iOS simulators by itself.
Cannot verify App Store approval outcomes or current review decisions.
Requires project context for precise code changes and architecture advice.
May need updated Apple documentation for newly released APIs.

use_cases.title: Design a New iOS Feature
use_cases.description: Plan screens, state, data flow, accessibility, and error handling before implementation.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy UIKit Code
use_cases.description: Identify safe migration paths from UIKit components to SwiftUI while preserving behavior.

use_cases.title: Prepare for App Store Release
use_cases.description: Review privacy, testing, performance, metadata, and distribution steps before submission.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a SwiftUI Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a SwiftUI screen for [feature]. Include state management, navigation, loading states, errors, and accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: Review iOS Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this iOS architecture for [app type]. Recommend improvements for MVVM, dependency injection, persistence, and testability.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Platform Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide implementation of [Apple framework] in my iOS app. Cover permissions, data flow, privacy, testing, and fallback behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my iOS app release plan for App Store review, privacy labels, TestFlight, performance, crash reporting, and accessibility.

output_examples.input: Plan a SwiftUI onboarding flow with account creation and consent screens.
output_examples.output: A screen-by-screen plan with navigation state, validation rules, privacy prompts, accessibility checks, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: Review my data layer for Core Data and CloudKit synchronization.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering model ownership, conflict handling, offline behavior, migration risks, and recommended tests.

output_examples.input: Prepare my app for TestFlight and App Store submission.
output_examples.output: A release checklist covering builds, signing, metadata, privacy labels, crash reporting, screenshots, and review risks.

best_practices: Provide the target iOS version, device classes, and Apple frameworks before asking for implementation guidance.
Ask for testing, accessibility, privacy, and performance checks with every production feature.
Share relevant project structure or constraints when requesting architecture recommendations.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic iOS code without stating the app context and deployment target.
Do not skip privacy and permission behavior when using Apple platform services.
Do not treat App Store guidance as a guarantee of review approval.

faq.question: Can this skill help with both SwiftUI and UIKit?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on SwiftUI-first development and includes UIKit integration and migration guidance.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready Swift code?
faq.answer: It can provide implementation guidance and Swift examples, but project-specific review and testing remain required.

faq.question: Can it help with App Store optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers metadata, screenshots, privacy labels, TestFlight, and App Store review considerations.

faq.question: Does it replace Apple documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use current Apple documentation for newly released APIs and final platform requirements.

faq.question: Can it review iOS security and privacy choices?
faq.answer: Yes. It can discuss Keychain use, ATS, certificate pinning, privacy prompts, and secure coding practices.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
